description: A service intended as a singleton is provided at multiple levels, so components silently get separate instances and split state.
body: Failure mode: a service is added to several components' providers arrays (or both root and a component) while the code assumes one shared instance, so each subtree gets its own instance and state written in one is invisible in another. Detection signals: a value set via the service in one component is not seen in another; constructor/instance counters fire more than once; the same token is provided at multiple injector levels. Do not provide a shared service at multiple levels. Safer fallback: provide app-wide singletons with providedIn: 'root', reserve component/route providers for deliberately scoped instances, and assert the expected instance count in a test.
